body.title: [sprint] Consequence check: Igor replies reach web UI
body.intention: I intend that Igor replies posted to the web routing channel are visibly delivered in the web UI without manual tmux inspection.
body.description:
D-igor-web-reply-2026-06-01 shipped T-igor-web-reply-routing (closed), but no consequence check was ever run.

**What changed:** T-igor-web-reply-routing wired Igor's reply path to the web server so replies appear in the web UI.

**Predicted unintended effects:** Reply routing may have shipped as a mechanism but the web UI may not render incoming replies (wrong channel, wrong author filter, missing WebSocket push).

**Signals to watch:** Send a message to Igor from the web UI. Does a reply appear in the web UI within a reasonable time? Check: tmux console shows reply but web UI doesn't (routing gap), or neither (Igor not responding).

**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-06-23

**Affected files:** None — observation and verification only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe whether Igor replies reach the web UI chat pane. Report: working / broken with reason / partial.
**Test plan:** no tests — live observation ticket
**Completion criteria:** Manually send a message from web UI; observe whether Igor's reply appears in the web UI. Record outcome as: working / broken (reason) / partial.
